query:
your thoughts on 448 scope? SCOPE.md for vnc-012 — product/features/vnc-012/SCOPE.md                                            
                                                                                                      
  Problem: Agents emit "3770" (string) where rmcp expects 3770 (integer), causing invalid type: string
   "3770", expected i64 failures on 9 fields across 5 param structs.                                  
                                                                  
  Approach: Create crates/unimatrix-server/src/mcp/serde_util.rs with 3 helpers                       
  (deserialize_i64_or_string, deserialize_opt_i64_or_string, deserialize_opt_usize_or_string), apply
  #[serde(deserialize_with)] + #[schemars(with = "i64")] to all 9 affected fields, add unit tests.    
                                                                  
  12 acceptance criteria covering: string inputs accepted, integer inputs unchanged, non-numeric      
  strings rejected, JSON Schema stays type: integer.
                                                                                                      
  Two open questions for you:                                                                         
   
  1. Q2: Should the helpers live in a new mcp/serde_util.rs submodule (researcher recommends this,    
  consistent with existing response/ submodule pattern), or as private helpers inline in tools.rs?
  2. Q3: For evidence_limit: Option<usize> on 32-bit targets — silently truncate values > u32::MAX, or
   defensively reject them? (production is 64-bit, so this is mostly academic)

entries.title: How to add an integer parameter to an existing MCP tool in unimatrix-server
ADR-007: New ServerError Variants for Validation and Scanning
ADR-001: Deserializer Helpers in mcp/serde_util.rs Submodule
Custom serde deserializer + schemars schema override for MCP tool param structs
